creator: DRowe@csnstores.com
text:
Thanks for the heads up.
I need to take a look at what was going wrong on my test box that was causi=
ng that not to work.

Just tried it on a separate box and you're absolutely correct.

Will be reading through the rc.subr script/man to see what other magic vari=
ables are available.
http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=3Drc.subr&apropos=3D0&sektion=3D0&=
manpath=3DFreeBSD+8.2-RELEASE&format=3Dhtml

Thanks,
-Dan
________________________________________
From: Sahil Tandon [sahil@FreeBSD.org]
Sent: Tuesday, March 01, 2011 10:29 PM
To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org; bsdports
Subject: Re: ports/154571: [PATCH] databases/memcached: memcached_flags not=
 used

This patch is not required.  ${name}_flags are automatically added by
/etc/rc.subr, and the empty variable assignment is unnecessary and
discouraged.  I cannot reproduce your problem.  Please let us know if it
is still an issue for you, as this PR will be closed in a few days.

Example:

 # grep memcached /etc/rc.conf
 memcached_enable=3D"YES"
 memcached_flags=3D"-m 2"

 # /usr/local/etc/rc.d/memcached start
 Starting memcached.

 # ps aux | grep memcached
 nobody 36923  0.0  0.2 11296  1984  ??  Is   10:26PM   0:00.01 /usr/local/=
bin/memcached -m 2 -d -u nobody

As you can see, the flags are indeed added, without changing the rc
script in any way.

--
Sahil Tandon <sahil@FreeBSD.org>
